title: The Hero with a Thousand Faces — Monomyth
author: Joseph Campbell
source: The Hero with a Thousand Faces, 1949. Synthesized from Jung (archetypes, the collective unconscious), Frazer, and especially Van Gennep's rites of passage (1909).

lineage.note: Synthesized from Jung, Frazer, Van Gennep. Hugely influential, also fairly criticized for cherry-picking and flattening cultural specificity. Vogler's adaptation (1992) is what actually runs Hollywood.

stages.name: The Call to Adventure
stages.function: Hero is called to leave the ordinary world.

stages.name: Supernatural Aid
stages.function: A mentor or guide appears, often with a gift.

stages.name: Crossing the First Threshold
stages.function: Hero commits to the adventure; enters the special world.

stages.name: Belly of the Whale
stages.function: The final separation from the known world; metaphorical death.

stages.name: The Road of Trials
stages.function: A series of tests, challenges, and ordeals.

stages.name: Meeting with the Goddess
stages.function: Hero encounters a powerful figure of love or unconditional acceptance.

stages.name: Woman as Temptress
stages.function: Temptation to abandon the quest. (Note: the name is Campbell's; the function is 'temptation toward easy gratification' and is not gendered.)

stages.name: Atonement with the Father
stages.function: Confrontation with the ultimate authority figure — often the source of the hero's fear.

stages.name: Apotheosis
stages.function: Hero achieves a higher state of being or understanding.

stages.name: The Ultimate Boon
stages.function: The goal of the quest is achieved.

stages.name: Refusal of the Return
stages.function: Hero reluctant to return to the ordinary world.

stages.name: The Magic Flight
stages.function: Escape with the boon, often pursued.

stages.name: Rescue from Without
stages.function: Hero needs help to return; the ordinary world reaches in.

stages.name: The Crossing of the Return Threshold
stages.function: Hero returns; integration of the two worlds.

stages.name: Master of Two Worlds
stages.function: Hero comfortable in both special and ordinary worlds.

stages.name: Freedom to Live
stages.function: Hero lives in the present, freed from fear of death; community is restored.

applicability.limitations: 17 stages is too many for routine assembly. The gendered names ('Meeting with the Goddess', 'Woman as Temptress') are dated and read awkwardly today — translate them by FUNCTION not by NAME. Critics (notably feminist + cultural-studies scholarship) argue Campbell flattened culturally specific myths into one Western-coded shape.
